title: Home
excerpt: Purdue SEARCH is a student-led space analog research organization at Purdue University. We run interdisciplinary programs spanning astronaut training, bioastronautics research, space habitat design, and outreach.

title: About SEARCH
excerpt: Founded in Summer 2022, SEARCH operates six semi-independent subteams focused on analog astronaut training, life support research, software development, and community outreach. Meet our officers and academic advisors.

title: Contact Us
excerpt: Get in touch with SEARCH via our contact form, join us at weekly meetings, or find us on Discord and Instagram. We meet weekly on Purdue's campus.

title: Blog & News
excerpt: Latest news and updates from Purdue SEARCH. Follow our programs, events, and research milestones throughout the academic year.

title: Outreach Events
excerpt: SEARCH hosts campus tabling events, speaker series, and film screenings. Past speakers include representatives from NASA JPL, Blue Origin, SETI, and Virgin Galactic.

title: SA²TP — Student Analog Astronaut Training Program
excerpt: The Student Analog Astronaut Training Program gives crew members real flight training in Cessna and Cirrus aircraft, skydiving certification, scuba diving, and participation in analog habitat simulations like ASTRO-USA.

title: SA²TP — Crew 1
excerpt: Crew 1 completed their analog astronaut training during Summer 2023, logging flight hours, skydive jumps, and open water scuba certification. Read about their mission experiences and milestones.

title: Rod Interview — Purdue Exponent
excerpt: SEARCH founder Rodrigo Schmitt discusses the origins of SEARCH, the vision for SA²TP, and why Purdue students are uniquely positioned to advance human spaceflight in an interview with the Purdue Exponent.

title: Research — Microgreen Microwaves
excerpt: SEARCH's research team investigates closed-loop food production for space habitats, including microgreen cultivation, hydroponics systems, and participation in NASA's Rasc-AL design competition.

title: NASA Rasc-AL Competition
excerpt: SEARCH competed in NASA's Revolutionary Aerospace Systems Concepts – Academic Linkage (Rasc-AL) competition, presenting a lunar habitat design concept with integrated life-support and food production systems.

title: Software — NASA SUITS Challenge
excerpt: The SEARCH software team develops augmented reality interfaces for astronaut telemetry using Microsoft HoloLens, competing in NASA's SUITS (Spacesuit User Interface Technologies for Students) Challenge.

title: SUITS — Project Details
excerpt: SEARCH presented their SUITS AR interface at Johnson Space Center. The system overlays real-time life-support telemetry onto an astronaut's field of view using HoloLens 2 headsets.

title: Analog Programs
excerpt: SEARCH participates in field research at analog environments including Kennedy Space Center and Biosphere 2 in Arizona to simulate deep-space mission conditions.

title: Astro-USA Habitat
excerpt: ASTRO-USA (Analog Space Technology for Research & Operations – United States of America) is SEARCH's flagship closed-loop life-support habitat built from a modified shipping container, supporting multi-day crew simulations.

title: Astro-USA — Program Overview
excerpt: An overview of the ASTRO-USA program goals, crew mission structure, and how the habitat integrates water recycling, food production, waste management, and atmospheric control for analog astronaut missions.

title: Astro-USA — Habitat Architecture
excerpt: The ASTRO-USA habitat architecture section details the structural design, subsystem layout, airlock, crew quarters, lab, and kitchen areas within the modified shipping container habitat.

title: Astro-USA — Hydroponics & LEAF
excerpt: ASTRO-USA's hydroponics bay uses NFT (Nutrient Film Technique) towers with automated pH and EC control to grow food crops for crew consumption, closing the nutrition loop within the habitat.
